outcome: All-cause mortality up to 12 months' follow-up
source_quote: Two patients, both in the 3-month follow-up phase, died while enrolled (one in CBT, one in EX), from causes not related to the study.

outcome: Health-related quality of life (MLWHF) up to 12 months' follow-up
source_quote: Table 3: MLHFQ at 24 weeks - CBT/EX 24.2+/-16.3 (n=15), UC 28.9+/-29.9 (n=14). Lower scores indicate better perceived HRQOL.

outcome: Hospital admissions (all-cause) up to 12 months' follow-up
source_quote: During the study period, 3 patients from the untrained group experienced an impairment of symptoms and were hospitalized.

outcome: Health-related quality of life (all scales) up to 12 months' follow-up
source_quote: Quality of life evaluated by WHOQOL-BREF questionnaire. Data reported only as percentage improvement (Physical: T 23+/-4% vs S 2+/-1%; Psychological: T 20+/-2% vs S 1+/-1%; Social: T 16+/-1% vs S 3+/-2%; Environmental: T 15+/-2% vs S 2+/-1%), not as raw mean/SD scores. Figure 2 shows graphical data only.

outcome: Health-related quality of life (MLWHF) up to 12 months' follow-up
source_quote: Table 3: MLHFQ 4 months after post-test (6 months after discharge) - Exp 42.32 (8.83), Con 49.63 (12.39). Fb=8.272, P=.005. Analysed n=42 experimental, n=41 control.

outcome: Health-related quality of life (all scales) up to 12 months' follow-up
source_quote: Table 3: MLHFQ 4 months after post-test (6 months after discharge) - Exp 42.32 (8.83), Con 49.63 (12.39). MLHFQ is the only QOL scale used in this study.
